task: What tasks do I have due October 20 2023 in Tasks app? Answer with the titles only. If there are multiples titles, format your answer in a comma separated list.

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the phone's home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Phone, Messages, Chrome, and another Gmail icon in the dock. The "Tasks" app is not visible on this screen.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app → | Step 2: Navigate to the tasks scheduled for Friday | Step 3: Extract the titles of these tasks | Step 4: Format the titles into a comma-separated list and provide the answer

[Decision]
The goal is to check tasks due on Friday in the Tasks app. Since I am on the home screen and the Tasks app is not visible among the shortcuts, I need to open it. I will use the system's open app function to launch "Tasks" directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The screen displays the "My Tasks" list. I can see tasks categorized by time: "Overdue" (Take Dog to the Vet - Mon), "Tue" (Research Vacation Destinations), "Wed" (Renew Driver's License), "Thu" (Brainstorm Blog Post Ideas), "Yest" (Call Grandma, Brainstorm Blog Post Ideas), and sections for "Due today" (Attend Team Meeting) and "Due tomorrow". The tasks due on Friday are not currently visible on the screen.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to the tasks scheduled for Friday → | Step 3: Extract the titles of these tasks | Step 4: Format the titles into a comma-separated list and provide the answer

[Decision]
The current step in the plan requires finding tasks scheduled for Friday. Since the current view only shows tasks up to "Due tomorrow" and earlier ones, I need to scroll down the page to reveal the tasks scheduled for Friday. I will perform a swipe up action to view the rest of the list.

[Memory]
Target: Tasks due Friday (titles only, comma-separated list). Current visible list ends at "Due tomorrow".

[Observation]
The screen shows the "Tasks" list. I can see tasks categorized by due dates. Under "Due Friday", there are two visible tasks: "Follow up on support tickets" and "Review quarterly goals". The list continues downwards, and there is another task partially visible at the bottom edge starting with "Plan team...".

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to the tasks scheduled for Friday ✓ | Step 3: Extract the titles of these tasks → | Step 4: Format the titles into a comma-separated list and provide the answer

[Decision]
The current step requires extracting all titles for Friday tasks. I have found two tasks under "Due Friday" ("Follow up on support tickets" and "Review quarterly goals"), but there is another task starting at the bottom of the screen ("Plan team...") that is cut off. To ensure I don't miss any tasks due Friday, I need to scroll down further to see the full list under this section. I will perform another swipe up action.

[Memory]
Target: Tasks due Friday (titles only, comma-separated list). Found tasks so far: "Follow up on support tickets", "Review quarterly goals". Need to check if there are more under "Due Friday".

[Observation]
The screen displays the Tasks app list. Under the "Due Friday" section, I can now see three complete tasks: "Follow up on support tickets", "Review quarterly goals", and "Plan team outing". The list ends with a "Completed" section below it.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open the Tasks app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to the tasks scheduled for Friday ✓ | Step 3: Extract the titles of these tasks ✓ | Step 4: Format the titles into a comma-separated list and provide the answer →

[Decision]
Based on the observation, I have successfully identified all tasks due Friday: "Follow up on support tickets", "Review quarterly goals", and "Plan team outing". The plan's current step is to format these titles into a comma-separated list and provide the answer to the user. I will use the Speak action to output the final formatted list as requested by the original instruction.

[Memory]
Target: Tasks due Friday (titles only, comma-separated list). Final extracted tasks: "Follow up on support tickets", "Review quarterly goals", "Plan team outing". Ready to output.

arguments.text: Follow up on support tickets, Review quarterly goals, Plan team outing
